verses.text: Against Ammon, who gloated 'Aha' over the profaned sanctuary; God will give them to the men of the east. Mockery judged. To rejoice at God's people's fall is to invite one's own.
Against Ammon, who gloated 'Aha' over the profaned sanctuary; God will give them to the men of the east. Mockery judged. To rejoice at God's people's fall is to invite one's own.
Against Ammon, who gloated 'Aha' over the profaned sanctuary; God will give them to the men of the east. Mockery judged. To rejoice at God's people's fall is to invite one's own.
Because they clapped and stamped with malice against Israel, God stretches out His hand to destroy them. Spiteful joy repaid. God marks the secret gladness of an enemy's heart.
Because they clapped and stamped with malice against Israel, God stretches out His hand to destroy them. Spiteful joy repaid. God marks the secret gladness of an enemy's heart.
Because they clapped and stamped with malice against Israel, God stretches out His hand to destroy them. Spiteful joy repaid. God marks the secret gladness of an enemy's heart.
Because they clapped and stamped with malice against Israel, God stretches out His hand to destroy them. Spiteful joy repaid. God marks the secret gladness of an enemy's heart.
Against Moab, who said Judah is 'like unto all the heathen'; God will open her frontier to judgment. Contempt answered. To deny that God's people are special is to despise Him.
Against Moab, who said Judah is 'like unto all the heathen'; God will open her frontier to judgment. Contempt answered. To deny that God's people are special is to despise Him.
Against Moab, who said Judah is 'like unto all the heathen'; God will open her frontier to judgment. Contempt answered. To deny that God's people are special is to despise Him.
Against Moab, who said Judah is 'like unto all the heathen'; God will open her frontier to judgment. Contempt answered. To deny that God's people are special is to despise Him.
Against Edom, who took cruel vengeance on Judah; God will lay His vengeance by the hand of Israel. Revenge requited. The Lord repays those who repay His people in malice.
Against Edom, who took cruel vengeance on Judah; God will lay His vengeance by the hand of Israel. Revenge requited. The Lord repays those who repay His people in malice.
Against Edom, who took cruel vengeance on Judah; God will lay His vengeance by the hand of Israel. Revenge requited. The Lord repays those who repay His people in malice.
Against the Philistines, for old hatred and revenge; God will execute 'great vengeance,' and they shall know Him. Long grudges judged. Cherished hatred ripens into ruin.
